title: HTML Headings
content:
### HTML Headings Headings are titles and subtitles for your content. There are **six levels**: `

` to `

`. --- ### Heading Tags ```html

Heading 1 (most important)

Heading 2

Heading 3

Heading 4

Heading 5
Heading 6 (least important)
``` --- ### Importance vs Size | Tag | Importance | Default Size | |-----|-----------|-------------| | `

` | Highest | 2em (largest) | | `

` | High | 1.5em | | `

` | Medium | 1.17em | | `

` | Normal | 1em | | `

` | Low | 0.83em | | `
` | Lowest | 0.67em (smallest) | --- ### SEO and Accessibility - Search engines use headings to understand page structure - Screen readers use headings for navigation - Use **one** `

` per page (the main title) - Do **not** skip heading levels (h1, then h2, not h4) ```html

My Blog

Today's Post

Introduction

Main Content

Comments

``` --- ### Styling Headings Headings can be styled with CSS: ```html

Big Centered Heading

``` > **Best Practice:** Do not use headings just for bold or large text. Use them for actual headings. Use CSS (`font-size`, `font-weight`) for visual styling.
codeExample:
Main Title (h1)

Section Title (h2)

Subsection Title (h3)

Minor Title (h4)

Small Title (h5)
Tiny Title (h6)

quiz.question: Which is the most important heading tag?
